CS143笔记

环境搭建​

以下是所有相关文档：

  • 作业文档： CS143
  • 作业材料： Anarion-zuo/Stanford-CS143
  • 中文EDX教学视频： CS-143 斯坦福编译原理(中文翻译)
  • Flex：Lexical Analysis With Flex, for Flex 2.6.2
  • Bison: Bison Manual
  • Cool Reference: Manual：Cool Reference Manual
  • Tour of the Cool Support Code: Tour of the Cool Support Code
  • 测试数据：stanford-lagunita-cs1-compilers-grading-scripts

使用docker搭建环境，使用的image是这个 syhkiller/stanford-cs143

详细的环境配置请参考网页简介

  • 拉取image docker pull syhkiller/stanford-cs143
  • 运行container docker run -it --name cs143 b3afe36e3c78 /bin/bash

Note​

有限自动机​

自动机的本质与状态转移图类似，

  • 有限自动机是识别器，它只能对每个可能的输入串简单地回答“是”或“否”。
  • 有限自动机分为两类：非确定性有限自动机（NFA）和确定性有限自动机（DFA）。

非确定性有限自动机 (NFA)​

NFA对边上的标签没有限制，空串 ε\varepsilonε 也可以作为标签。

一个NFA由下面几个部分组成

  • 一个有限的状态集合 S{S}S
  • 一个有限的输入符号集合 Σ{\Sigma}Σ
  • 一个有限的转移函数集合 δ{\delta}δ ，它为每个状态和 Σ∪{ε}\Sigma \cup \{\varepsilon\}Σ∪{ε} 中的每个符号都给出相应的后继状态
  • 一个初始状态 s0{s_0}s0​
  • 一个接受状态集合 F{F}F

确定性有限自动机 (DFA)​

DFA是NFA的一个特例，它的转移函数集合 δ{\delta}δ 只能有一个标签，且不能有空串 ε\varepsilonε 。

上下文无关文法​

LL(1)​

考虑一个非终结符 A{A}A ，产生式 A→α{A \rightarrow \alpha}A→α ，Token t{t}t

要让产生式推导成功，在解析表中需要满足 ParsingTable[A,t]=α\text{ParsingTable}[A,t] = \alphaParsingTable[A,t]=α

First集​
α→∗tβ\alpha \rightarrow^{*} t \betaα→∗tβ

我们称 t∈First(α){t \in \text{First}(\alpha)}t∈First(α)，定义 First(α)\text{First}(\alpha)First(α) 集合为可从 α\alphaα 推导得到的串的首符号的集合，即

First(X)={t∣X→∗tα}∪{ε∣X→∗ε}\text{First}(X) = \{ t | X \rightarrow^{*} t \alpha \} \cup \{ \varepsilon | X \rightarrow^{*} \varepsilon \}First(X)={t∣X→∗tα}∪{ε∣X→∗ε}

一些直接的推导

First(α)⊆First(X)    when    X→A1...Anα    and    ε∈First(Ai)\begin{aligned} & \text{First}(\alpha) \subseteq \text{First}(X) \\ {\,\,\,\,}when{\,\,\,\,} & X \rightarrow A_1...A_n\alpha {\,\,\,\,}and{\,\,\,\,} \varepsilon \in \text{First}(A_i) \end{aligned}when​First(α)⊆First(X)X→A1​...An​αandε∈First(Ai​)​
Follow集​
Follow(X)={t∣S→∗βXtδ}\text{Follow}(X) = \{t | S \rightarrow^{*} \beta X t \delta \}Follow(X)={t∣S→∗βXtδ}

Follow(X){\text{Follow}(X)}Follow(X) 定义为可能在某些句型中紧跟在 XXX 右边的终结符的集合，如果 XXX 是某些句型的最右边的符号，那么 XXX 的 Follow(X){\text{Follow}(X)}Follow(X) 集合中还包含 ${\$}$

一些直接的推导

X→AB⇒First(B)⊆Follow(A)⇒Follow(X)⊆Follow(B)if    B→∗ε⇒Follow(X)⊆Follow(A)\begin{aligned} X \rightarrow AB &\Rightarrow \text{First}(B) \subseteq \text{Follow}(A) \\ &\Rightarrow \text{Follow}(X) \subseteq \text{Follow}(B) \\ \text{if}{\,\,\,\,} B \rightarrow^{*}\varepsilon &\Rightarrow \text{Follow}(X) \subseteq \text{Follow}(A) \\ \end{aligned}X→ABifB→∗ε​⇒First(B)⊆Follow(A)⇒Follow(X)⊆Follow(B)⇒Follow(X)⊆Follow(A)​
LL(1)解析表​

如果我们要为一个上下文无关文法G构造一个LL(1)解析表ParsingTable，对于每一个产生式 A→α{A \rightarrow \alpha}A→α

  • 对于每一个终结符 t∈First(α)t \in \text{First}(\alpha)t∈First(α) 有 ParsingTable[A,t]=α{\text{ParsingTable}[A,t] = \alpha}ParsingTable[A,t]=α
  • 如果 ε∈First(α)\varepsilon \in \text{First}(\alpha)ε∈First(α) ，对于每一个 t∈Follow(A)t \in \text{Follow}(A)t∈Follow(A) 有 ParsingTable[A,t]=α{\text{ParsingTable}[A,t] = \alpha}ParsingTable[A,t]=α
  • 如果 ε∈First(α)\varepsilon \in \text{First}(\alpha)ε∈First(α) 并且 $∈Follow(A)\$ \in \text{Follow}(A)$∈Follow(A) ，则有 ParsingTable[A,$]=α{\text{ParsingTable}[A,\$] = \alpha}ParsingTable[A,$]=α
LL(1)文法特征​
  • 无左递归
  • 可以提取左公因式
  • 不允许语义混淆

大多数编程语言都不是LL(1)文法的

LR(1)​

Assignment​

CS143一共有四个PA，EDX上会多出一个熟悉 Cool 语言的前置PA1，PA2-5对应CS143的PA1-4

Programming Assignment 1 | 熟悉Cool语言​

完成该实验必须先看完Cool Reference Manual前十节以及视频的4、5、6节，熟悉 Cool 语言的基本语法

该实验要求实现一个 Cool 语言的栈，完成指定的命令

coolc 是Cool 语言的编译器，SPIM 是MIPS虚拟环境

代码
  • 代码
  • 结果
(*
 *  CS164 Fall 94
 *
 *  Programming Assignment 1
 *    Implementation of a simple stack machine.
 *
 *  Skeleton file
 *)

class StackItem {
   -- get number
   getNumber() : Int { 0 };
   -- get '+' 's' 'e' 'd' 'x'
   getChar() : String { "Get a command" };
   -- judge input option
   execute(n : Stack) : Stack {
      let ret : Stack in {
         (new IO).out_string("Error: Undefined command!\n");
         ret;
      }
   };

};

class CommandInt inherits StackItem {
   number : Int;

   init(num : Int) : CommandInt {
      {
         number <- num;
         self;
      }
   };

   getNumber() : Int { number };
   getChar() : String { (new A2I).i2a(number) };

};

class CommandPlus inherits StackItem {
   init() : CommandPlus {
      self
   };

   execute(n : Stack) : Stack {
      let n1 : Stack <- n.getNext(),
          n2 : Stack <- n1.getNext(),
          sum : Int,
          ret : Stack in {
               if (not (isvoid n1)) then {
                  if (not (isvoid n2)) then {
                     -- calculate sum = n1 + n2
                     sum <- n1.top().getNumber() + n2.top().getNumber();
                     -- push sum
                     ret <- (new Stack).init((new CommandInt).init(sum), n2.getNext());
                  }
                  else
                     0
                  fi;
               }
               else
                  0
               fi;
               ret;
          }
   };

   getChar() : String { "+" };
};

class CommandSwap inherits StackItem {
   init() : CommandSwap {
      self
   };

   execute(n : Stack) : Stack {
      let next : Stack <- n.getNext().getNext() in {
         n <- n.getNext();
         n.setNext(next.getNext());
         next.setNext(n);
         next;
      }
   };

   getChar() : String { "s" };
};

class Stack{
   -- declare the structure of stack
   item : StackItem;
   next : Stack;

   init(i : StackItem, n : Stack) : Stack {
      {
         item <- i;
         next <- n;
         self;
      }
   };

   -- push an new item on the top of stack
   push(i : StackItem) : Stack {
      let newItem : Stack in {
         newItem <- (new Stack).init(i, self);
         newItem;
      }
   };

   -- get item from the top of stack
   top() : StackItem { item };

   -- get next item
   getNext() : Stack { next };

   -- set next item
   setNext(n : Stack) : Stack { next <- n };

};

class Main inherits A2I {

   stackTop : Stack;

   pushCommand(command : StackItem) : StackItem {
      {
         if (isvoid stackTop) then {
            let nil : Stack in {
               stackTop <- (new Stack).init(command, nil);
            };
         }
         else {
            stackTop <- stackTop.push(command);
         }
         fi;
         command;
      }
   };

   printStack() : Object {
      let n : Stack <- stackTop in {
         while (not (isvoid n)) loop {
            (new IO).out_string(n.top().getChar().concat("\n"));
            n <- n.getNext();
         } pool;
      }
   };

   executeCommand(command : String) : Object {
      {
         if (command = "+") then
            pushCommand((new CommandPlus).init())
         else 
            if (command = "s") then
               pushCommand((new CommandSwap).init())
            else
               if (command = "d") then
                  printStack()
               else
                  if (command = "e") then
                     let n : Stack <- stackTop in {
                        if (not (isvoid n)) then
                           stackTop <- n.top().execute(n)
                        else
                           0
                        fi;
                     }
                  else
                     pushCommand((new CommandInt).init((new A2I).a2i(command)))
                  fi
               fi
            fi
         fi;
      }
   };

   main() : Object {
      let inputString : String in {
         (new IO).out_string(">");
         inputString <- (new IO).in_string();
         while(not (inputString = "x")) loop {
            executeCommand(inputString);
            (new IO).out_string(">");
            inputString <- (new IO).in_string();
         } pool;
         -- (new IO).out_string("COOL program successfully executed\n");
      }
   };

};
╭─root@82017536e4ec ~/cool/assignments/PA1 
╰─# coolc stack.cl atoi.cl
╭─root@82017536e4ec ~/cool/assignments/PA1 
╰─# spim -file stack.s    
SPIM Version 8.0 of January 8, 2010
Copyright 1990-2010, James R. Larus.
All Rights Reserved.
See the file README for a full copyright notice.
Loaded: /usr/class/cs143/lib/trap.handler
The following symbols are undefined:
main


>1
>+
>2
>s
>d
s
2
+
1
>e
>e
>d
3
>x
COOL program successfully executed

Programming Assignment 2 | 词法分析​

完成该实验同样需要阅读 Cool 语法，同时需要阅读 Flex 文档

实验目的是通过Flex对 Cool 语言进行词法分析

flex文件的结构在PA1文档中有清楚的描述

%{
Declarations
%}
Definitions
%%
Rules
%%
User subroutines

flex 一共分为三个部分：

  • 声明：包括Declarations和Definitions，前者是C声明，后者是flex的定义
  • 规则：在这里编写正则表达式来匹配token
  • 代码：在这里编写用户代码

这个实验需要做的就是编写 cool.flex 文件来进行词法分析，标准输出参考 reflexer test.cl

我们需要对文本分析出以下几个token：

  • Comment (注释)
  • Keyword (关键字)
  • Operator (操作符)
  • String constants (字符串常量)
  • Identifier (标识)
  • White space (空格及特殊符)

Comment​

Cool语言中分两种注释

  • 以--开头的行内注释
  • 以(* ... *)为标识的注释块

对于第一种行内注释，我们直接识别出--，进入行内注释状态，不匹配之后的内容直到遇到\n

<INITIAL>"--" {
  inline_comment_caller = INITIAL;
  BEGIN(INLINE_COMMENT);
}

<INLINE_COMMENT>[^\n]* {}
<INLINE_COMMENT>\n {
  curr_lineno++;
  BEGIN(inline_comment_caller);
}

对于第二种注释块，它以(*开头，我们直接识别出(*，进入注释块状态，不匹配之后的内容直到遇到*)

这中间有几个问题需要解决

  1. 如果遇到Keyword
  2. 如果注释块内有多个注释块，即嵌套注释

我们的解决方法是：除了\n和(*之外的都不匹配，同时上一步匹配到的去除掉特例

"(*" {
  comment_caller = INITIAL;
  comment_layer++;
  BEGIN(COMMENT);
}
<COMMENT>[^\n(*]* { }

<COMMENT>[(*)] { }

<COMMENT>"*)" {
  comment_layer--;
  if (comment_layer == 0) {
    BEGIN(comment_caller);
  }
}

<COMMENT><<EOF>> {
  yylval.error_msg = "EOF in comment";
  BEGIN(comment_caller);
  return (ERROR);
}

"*)" {
  yylval.error_msg = "Unmatched *)";
  return (ERROR);
}

Keyword​

这里需要注意的是，在文档中说明了：

引用

Except for the constants true and false, keywords are case insensitive. To conform to the rules for other objects, the first letter of true and false must be lowercase; the trailing letters may be upper or lower case.

所以Cool语言的关键字对大小写不敏感，且true和false只要保证第一个字符是小写即可，匹配规则如下

(?i:class) { return CLASS; }

(?i:else) { return ELSE; }

(?i:fi) { return FI; }

(?i:if) { return IF; }

(?i:in) { return IN; }

(?i:inherits) { return INHERITS; }

(?i:let) { return LET; }

(?i:loop) { return LOOP; }

(?i:pool) { return POOL; }

(?i:then) { return THEN; }

(?i:while) { return WHILE; }

(?i:case) { return CASE; }

(?i:esac) { return ESAC; }

(?i:of) { return OF; }

(?i:new) { return NEW; }

(?i:isvoid) { return ISVOID; }

(?i:not) { return NOT; }

t(?i:rue) {
    cool_yylval.boolean = 1;
    return BOOL_CONST;
}

f(?i:alse) {
    cool_yylval.boolean = 0;
    return BOOL_CONST;
}
提示

在实际匹配的时候，有可能会出现一个Token既属于Keyword，也属于Identifier。这里要根据正则表达式的规则：Choose the one listed first. 即优先匹配第一个列出的。所以要将Keyword的匹配放在Identifier前

Operator​

单独对每个操作符进行匹配即可

"<-" { return ASSIGN; }

"<=" { return LE; }

"=>" { return DARROW; }

"+" { return int('+'); }

"-" { return int('-'); }

"*" { return int('*'); }

"/" { return int('/'); }

"<" { return int('<'); }

"=" { return int('='); }

"." { return int('.'); }

";" { return int(';'); }

"~" { return int('~'); }

"{" { return int('{'); }

"}" { return int('}'); }

"(" { return int('('); }

")" { return int(')'); }

":" { return int(':'); }

"@" { return int('@'); }

"," { return int(','); }

String constants​

String constants只能是双引号""括起来的字符串常量，所以只用匹配"，再进入字符串匹配状态

yymore()的作用是将下一个token包含当前的token

引用

A string may not contain EOF. A string may not contain the null (character \0). Any other character may be included in a string. Strings cannot cross file boundaries.

所以进入字符串匹配状态之后，不匹配非法字符，直到遇见"

此时yytext存储的就是整个字符串，通过匹配字符串中的\来判断如何添加特殊字符

(\") {
  string_caller = INITIAL;
  BEGIN(STRING);
  yymore();
}

<STRING>[^\\\"\n]* {
  yymore();
}

<STRING>\\[^\n] {
  yymore();
}

<STRING>\\\n {
  curr_lineno++;
  yymore();
}

<STRING><<EOF>> {
  yylval.error_msg = "EOF in string constant";
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  yyrestart(yyin);
  return (ERROR);
}

<STRING>\n {
  yylval.error_msg = "Unterminated string constant";
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  return (ERROR);
}

<STRING>\\0 {
  yylval.error_msg = "Unterminated string constant";
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  return (ERROR);
}

<STRING>\" {
  std::string input(yytext, yyleng);
  input = input.substr(1, input.size() - 2);

  std::string output;
  std::string::size_type pos;

  if (input.find_first_of('\0') != std::string::npos) {
    cool_yylval.error_msg = "String contains null character";
    BEGIN(string_caller);
    return (ERROR);
  }

  while ((pos = input.find_first_of("\\")) != std::string::npos) {
    output += input.substr(0, pos);

    switch(input[pos + 1]) {
      case 'b':
        output += "\b";
        break;
      case 't':
        output += "\t";
        break;
      case 'n':
        output += "\n";
        break;
      case 'f':
        output += "\f";
        break;
      default:
        output += input[pos + 1];
        break;
    }

    input = input.substr(pos + 2, input.size() - 2);
  }

  output += input;

  if (output.length() > 1024) {
      yylval.error_msg = "String constant too long";
      BEGIN 0;
      return ERROR;    
  }

  cool_yylval.symbol = stringtable.add_string((char *)output.c_str());
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  return (STR_CONST);
}

Identifier and Integer​

引用

Integers are non-empty strings of digits 0-9. Identifiers are strings (other than keywords) consisting of letters, digits, and the underscore character. Type identifiers begin with a capital letter; object identifiers begin with a lower case letter. There are two other identifiers, self and SELF TYPE that are treated specially by Cool but are not treated as keywords. The special syntactic symbols (e.g., parentheses, assignment operator, etc.) are given in Figure 1.

在Cool语言中，整数是0-9的非空字符串，标识符是由字母、数字和下划线组成的字符串

Type identifier 以大写字母开头，Object identifer 以小写字母开头

[0-9]+ {
  cool_yylval.symbol = inttable.add_string(yytext);
  return (INT_CONST);
}

[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]* {
  cool_yylval.symbol = idtable.add_string(yytext);
  return (TYPEID);
}

[a-z][A-Za-z0-9_]* {
  cool_yylval.symbol = idtable.add_string(yytext);
  return (OBJECTID);
}

White space​

匹配出空格、tab、newline等white space

[ \t\f\r\v]+ {}

"\n" { 
  ++curr_lineno;
}

其余报错信息参考PA1文档中的Error Handing小节内容

代码
  • 代码
  • 结果
/*
 *  The scanner definition for COOL.
 */

/*
 *  Stuff enclosed in %{ %} in the first section is copied verbatim to the
 *  output, so headers and global definitions are placed here to be visible
 * to the code in the file.  Don't remove anything that was here initially
 */
%{
#include <cool-parse.h>
#include <stringtab.h>
#include <utilities.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>

/* The compiler assumes these identifiers. */
#define yylval cool_yylval
#define yylex  cool_yylex

/* Max size of string constants */
#define MAX_STR_CONST 1025
#define YY_NO_UNPUT   /* keep g++ happy */

extern FILE *fin; /* we read from this file */

/* define YY_INPUT so we read from the FILE fin:
 * This change makes it possible to use this scanner in
 * the Cool compiler.
 */
#undef YY_INPUT
#define YY_INPUT(buf,result,max_size) \
    if ( (result = fread( (char*)buf, sizeof(char), max_size, fin)) < 0) \
        YY_FATAL_ERROR( "read() in flex scanner failed");

char string_buf[MAX_STR_CONST]; /* to assemble string constants */
char *string_buf_ptr;

extern int curr_lineno;
extern int verbose_flag;

extern YYSTYPE cool_yylval;

/*
 *  Add Your own definitions here
 */

static int comment_caller;
static int inline_comment_caller;
static int string_caller;

static int comment_layer = 0;

%}

/*
 * Define names for regular expressions here.
 */


DARROW          =>
%s              COMMENT
%s              INLINE_COMMENT
%x              STRING

%%

 /*
  *  Nested comments
  */

 /*
  * 单行注释
  */

 /*
  * 多行注释
  */

"(*" {
  comment_caller = INITIAL;
  comment_layer++;
  BEGIN(COMMENT);
}
<COMMENT>[^\n(*]* { }

<COMMENT>[(*)] { }

<COMMENT>"*)" {
  comment_layer--;
  if (comment_layer == 0) {
    BEGIN(comment_caller);
  }
}

<COMMENT><<EOF>> {
  yylval.error_msg = "EOF in comment";
  BEGIN(comment_caller);
  return (ERROR);
}

"*)" {
  yylval.error_msg = "Unmatched *)";
  return (ERROR);
}

<INITIAL>"--" {
  inline_comment_caller = INITIAL;
  BEGIN(INLINE_COMMENT);
}

<INLINE_COMMENT>[^\n]* {}
<INLINE_COMMENT>\n {
  curr_lineno++;
  BEGIN(inline_comment_caller);
}

 /*
  *  The multiple-character operators.
  */


 /*
  * Keywords are case-insensitive except for the values true and false,
  * which must begin with a lower-case letter.
  */

 /*
  *  String constants (C syntax)
  *  Escape sequence \c is accepted for all characters c. Except for 
  *  \n \t \b \f, the result is c.
  *
  */

(\") {
  string_caller = INITIAL;
  BEGIN(STRING);
  yymore();
}

<STRING>[^\\\"\n]* {
  yymore();
}

<STRING>\\[^\n] {
  yymore();
}

<STRING>\\\n {
  curr_lineno++;
  yymore();
}

<STRING><<EOF>> {
  yylval.error_msg = "EOF in string constant";
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  yyrestart(yyin);
  return (ERROR);
}

<STRING>\n {
  yylval.error_msg = "Unterminated string constant";
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  return (ERROR);
}

<STRING>\\0 {
  yylval.error_msg = "Unterminated string constant";
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  return (ERROR);
}

<STRING>\" {
  std::string input(yytext, yyleng);
  input = input.substr(1, input.size() - 2);

  std::string output;
  std::string::size_type pos;

  if (input.find_first_of('\0') != std::string::npos) {
    cool_yylval.error_msg = "String contains null character";
    BEGIN(string_caller);
    return (ERROR);
  }

  while ((pos = input.find_first_of("\\")) != std::string::npos) {
    output += input.substr(0, pos);

    switch(input[pos + 1]) {
      case 'b':
        output += "\b";
        break;
      case 't':
        output += "\t";
        break;
      case 'n':
        output += "\n";
        break;
      case 'f':
        output += "\f";
        break;
      default:
        output += input[pos + 1];
        break;
    }

    input = input.substr(pos + 2, input.size() - 2);
  }

  output += input;

  if (output.length() > 1024) {
      yylval.error_msg = "String constant too long";
      BEGIN 0;
      return ERROR;    
  }

  cool_yylval.symbol = stringtable.add_string((char *)output.c_str());
  BEGIN(string_caller);
  return (STR_CONST);
}

(?i:class) { return CLASS; }

(?i:else) { return ELSE; }

(?i:fi) { return FI; }

(?i:if) { return IF; }

(?i:in) { return IN; }

(?i:inherits) { return INHERITS; }

(?i:let) { return LET; }

(?i:loop) { return LOOP; }

(?i:pool) { return POOL; }

(?i:then) { return THEN; }

(?i:while) { return WHILE; }

(?i:case) { return CASE; }

(?i:esac) { return ESAC; }

(?i:of) { return OF; }

(?i:new) { return NEW; }

(?i:isvoid) { return ISVOID; }

(?i:not) { return NOT; }

t(?i:rue) {
    cool_yylval.boolean = 1;
    return BOOL_CONST;
}

f(?i:alse) {
    cool_yylval.boolean = 0;
    return BOOL_CONST;
}

[ \t\f\r\v]+ {}

[0-9]+ {
  cool_yylval.symbol = inttable.add_string(yytext);
  return (INT_CONST);
}

[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]* {
  cool_yylval.symbol = idtable.add_string(yytext);
  return (TYPEID);
}

[a-z][A-Za-z0-9_]* {
  cool_yylval.symbol = idtable.add_string(yytext);
  return (OBJECTID);
}

"\n" { 
  ++curr_lineno;
}

"<-" { return ASSIGN; }

"<=" { return LE; }

"=>" { return DARROW; }

"+" { return int('+'); }

"-" { return int('-'); }

"*" { return int('*'); }

"/" { return int('/'); }

"<" { return int('<'); }

"=" { return int('='); }

"." { return int('.'); }

";" { return int(';'); }

"~" { return int('~'); }

"{" { return int('{'); }

"}" { return int('}'); }

"(" { return int('('); }

")" { return int(')'); }

":" { return int(':'); }

"@" { return int('@'); }

"," { return int(','); }

[^\n] {
    yylval.error_msg = yytext;
    return ERROR;
}

%%
─root@82017536e4ec ~/cool/assignments/PA2 
╰─# perl pa1-grading.pl
Grading .....

flex -d -ocool-lex.cc cool.flex
cool.flex:167: warning, rule cannot be matched
/bin/sh -ec 'g++ -MM -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA2 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA2 cool-lex.cc | sed '\''s/\(cool-lex\.o\)[ :]*/\1 cool-lex.d : /g'\'' > cool-lex.d'
/bin/sh -ec 'g++ -MM -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA2 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA2 handle_flags.cc | sed '\''s/\(handle_flags\.o\)[ :]*/\1 handle_flags.d : /g'\'' > handle_flags.d'
/bin/sh -ec 'g++ -MM -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA2 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA2 stringtab.cc | sed '\''s/\(stringtab\.o\)[ :]*/\1 stringtab.d : /g'\'' > stringtab.d'
/bin/sh -ec 'g++ -MM -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA2 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA2 utilities.cc | sed '\''s/\(utilities\.o\)[ :]*/\1 utilities.d : /g'\'' > utilities.d'
/bin/sh -ec 'g++ -MM -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA2 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA2 lextest.cc | sed '\''s/\(lextest\.o\)[ :]*/\1 lextest.d : /g'\'' > lextest.d'

=====================================================================
You got a score of 63 out of 63.

Programming Assignment 3 | 语法分析​

完成本作业需要阅读完以下材料：

  • Cool Reference: Manual 第十、十一、十二小节：Cool Reference Manual
  • Tour of the Cool Support Code 第六小节: Tour of the Cool Support Code
  • Bison Manual前三小节: Bison Manual

PA2要求我们在PA1词法分析出来的Token流上使用Bison构建出AST

我们需要根据Cool语法写出上下文无关文法，这个在Cool的文档中就有

然后是Cool运算符优先级规定，也在Cool的文档中的11.1节中

需要注意的是Hand-out中的错误处理，

引用

You should use the error pseudo-nonterminal to add error handling capabilities in the parser. The purpose of error is to permit the parser to continue after some anticipated error. It is not a panacea and the parser may become completely confused. See the bison documentation for how best to use error. Your test file bad.cl should have some instances that illustrate the errors from which your parser can recover. To receive full credit, your parser should recover in at least the following situations:

  • If there is an error in a class definition but the class is terminated properly and the next class is syntactically correct, the parser should be able to restart at the next class definition.
  • Similarly, the parser should recover from errors in features (going on to the next feature), a let binding (going on to the next variable), and an expression inside a {...} block.

我们需要处理Class、feature、let、{expr}的错误，当遇到错误时，如果成功终止且之后的语法正确，继续执行之后的分析

代码
  • 代码
  • 结果
/*
*  cool.y
*              Parser definition for the COOL language.
*
*/
%{
    #include <iostream>
    #include "cool-tree.h"
    #include "stringtab.h"
    #include "utilities.h"

    extern char *curr_filename;
  
  
    /* Locations */
    #define YYLTYPE int              /* the type of locations */
      /* use the curr_lineno from the lexer
                                      for the location of tokens */
    
      extern int node_lineno;          /* set before constructing a tree node
                                        to whatever you want the line number
                                        for the tree node to be */
        
        
        #define YYLLOC_DEFAULT(Current, Rhs, N)         \
        Current = Rhs[1];                             \
        node_lineno = Current;
    
    
    #define SET_NODELOC(Current)  \
    node_lineno = Current;
    
    /*
    * IMPORTANT NOTE ON LINE NUMBERS
    * ********************************
    * The above definitions and macros cause every terminal in your grammar to 
    * have the line number supplied by the lexer. The only task you have to
    * implement for line numbers to work correctly, is to use SET_NODELOC()
    * before constructing any constructs from non-terminals in your grammar.
    * Example: Consider you are matching on the following very restrictive 
    * (fictional) construct that matches a plus between two integer constants. 
    * (SUCH A RULE SHOULD NOT BE  PART OF YOUR PARSER):
    
    * plus_consts   : INT_CONST '+' INT_CONST 
    
    * where INT_CONST is a terminal for an integer constant. Now, a correct
    * action for this rule that attaches the correct line number to plus_const
    * would look like the following:
    
    * plus_consts   : INT_CONST '+' INT_CONST 
    * {
    *   // Set the line number of the current non-terminal:
    *   // ***********************************************
    *   // You can access the line numbers of the i'th item with @i, just
    *   // like you acess the value of the i'th exporession with $i.
    *   //
    *   // Here, we choose the line number of the last INT_CONST (@3) as the
    *   // line number of the resulting expression (@$). You are free to pick
    *   // any reasonable line as the line number of non-terminals. If you 
    *   // omit the statement @$=..., bison has default rules for deciding which 
    *   // line number to use. Check the manual for details if you are interested.
    *   @$ = @3;
    *   
    *   
    *   // Observe that we call SET_NODELOC(@3); this will set the global variable
    *   // node_lineno to @3. Since the constructor call "plus" uses the value of 
    *   // this global, the plus node will now have the correct line number.
    *   SET_NODELOC(@3);
    *   
    *   // construct the result node:
    *   $$ = plus(int_const($1), int_const($3));
    * }
    */
    
    
    
    void yyerror(char *s);        /*  defined below; called for each parse error */
    extern int yylex();           /*  the entry point to the lexer  */
    
    /************************************************************************/
    /*                DONT CHANGE ANYTHING IN THIS SECTION                  */
    
    Program ast_root;         /* the result of the parse  */
    Classes parse_results;        /* for use in semantic analysis */
    int omerrs = 0;               /* number of errors in lexing and parsing */
%}
    
/*
* A union of all the types that can be the result of parsing actions.
*/

%union {
  Boolean boolean;
  Symbol symbol;
  Program program;
  Class_ class_;
  Classes classes;
  Feature feature;
  Features features;
  Formal formal;
  Formals formals;
  Case case_;
  Cases cases;
  Expression expression;
  Expressions expressions;
  char *error_msg;
}

/* 
*Declare the terminals; a few have types for associated lexemes.
*The token ERROR is never used in the parser; thus, it is a parse
*error when the lexer returns it.
*
*The integer following token declaration is the numeric constant used
*to represent that token internally.  Typically, Bison generates these
*on its own, but we give explicit numbers to prevent version parity
*problems (bison 1.25 and earlier start at 258, later versions -- at
*257)
*/
%token CLASS 258 ELSE 259 FI 260 IF 261 IN 262 
%token INHERITS 263 LET 264 LOOP 265 POOL 266 THEN 267 WHILE 268
%token CASE 269 ESAC 270 OF 271 DARROW 272 NEW 273 ISVOID 274
%token <symbol>  STR_CONST 275 INT_CONST 276 
%token <boolean> BOOL_CONST 277
%token <symbol>  TYPEID 278 OBJECTID 279 
%token ASSIGN 280 NOT 281 LE 282 ERROR 283

/*  DON'T CHANGE ANYTHING ABOVE THIS LINE, OR YOUR PARSER WONT WORK       */
/**************************************************************************/

/* Complete the nonterminal list below, giving a type for the semantic
value of each non terminal. (See section 3.6 in the bison 
documentation for details). */

/* Declare types for the grammar's non-terminals. */
%type <program> program
%type <classes> class_list
%type <class_> class

/* You will want to change the following line. */
%type <features> feature_list
%type <feature> feature
%type <formals> formal_list
%type <formal> formal
%type <cases> branch_list
%type <case_> branch
%type <expressions> expr_list
%type <expressions> expr_list_
%type <expression> expr
%type <expression> let

/* Precedence declarations go here. */
%left ASSIGN
%left NOT
%nonassoc LE '<' '='
%left '+' '-'
%left '*' '/'
%left ISVOID
%left '~'
%left '@'
%left '.'
    
%%
    /* 
    Save the root of the abstract syntax tree in a global variable.
    */
    /* program ::= [class;]+ */
    program:
      class_list
      {
        @$ = @1;
        ast_root = program($1);
      }
    ;
    /* [class;]+ */
    class_list:
      class
      {
        $$ = single_Classes($1);
        parse_results = $$;
      }
    | class_list class
      {
        $$ = append_Classes($1, single_Classes($2));
        parse_results = $$;
      }
    ;

    /* If no parent is specified, the class inherits from the Object class. */
    /* class ::= class TYPE [inherits TYPE] { [feature;]* } */
    class:
      CLASS TYPEID '{' feature_list '}' ';'
      {
        $$ = class_($2, idtable.add_string("Object"), $4, stringtable.add_string(curr_filename));
      }
    | CLASS TYPEID INHERITS TYPEID '{' feature_list '}' ';'
      {
        $$  = class_($2, $4, $6, stringtable.add_string(curr_filename));
      }
    | CLASS error ';' class
      {
        $$ = $4;
      }
    ;

    /* Feature list may be empty, but no empty features in list. */
    feature_list:
      %empty
      {
        $$ = nil_Features();
      }
    | feature_list feature
      {
        $$ = append_Features($1, single_Features($2));
      }
    | feature_list error ';'
      {
        $$ = $1;
      }
    ;

    feature:
      OBJECTID '(' ')' ':' TYPEID '{' expr '}' ';'
      {
        $$ = method($1, nil_Formals(), $5, $7);
      }
    | OBJECTID '(' formal_list ')' ':' TYPEID '{' expr '}' ';'
      {
        $$ = method($1, $3, $6, $8);
      }
    | OBJECTID ':' TYPEID ';'
      {
        $$ = attr($1, $3, no_expr());
      }
    | OBJECTID ':' TYPEID ASSIGN expr ';'
      {
        $$ = attr($1, $3, $5);
      }
    ;

    formal_list:
      formal
      {
        $$ = single_Formals($1);
      }
    | formal_list ',' formal
      {
        $$ = append_Formals($1, single_Formals($3));
      }
    ;

    formal:
      OBJECTID ':' TYPEID
      {
        $$ = formal($1, $3);
      }
    ;

    expr:
      OBJECTID ASSIGN expr
      {
        $$ = assign($1, $3);
      }
    | expr '.' OBJECTID '(' ')'
      {
        $$ = dispatch($1, $3, nil_Expressions());
      }
    | expr '.' OBJECTID '(' expr_list ')'
      {
        $$ = dispatch($1, $3, $5);
      }
    | expr '@' TYPEID '.' OBJECTID '(' ')'
      {
        $$ = static_dispatch($1, $3, $5, nil_Expressions());
      }
    | expr '@' TYPEID '.' OBJECTID '(' expr_list ')'
      {
        $$ = static_dispatch($1, $3, $5, $7);
      }
    | OBJECTID '(' ')'
      {
        $$ = dispatch(object(idtable.add_string("self")), $1, nil_Expressions());
      }
    | OBJECTID '(' expr_list ')'
      {
        $$ = dispatch(object(idtable.add_string("self")), $1, $3);
      }
    | IF expr THEN expr ELSE expr FI
      {
        $$ = cond($2, $4, $6);
      }
    | WHILE expr LOOP expr POOL
      {
        $$ = loop($2, $4);
      }
    | '{' expr_list_ '}'
      {
        $$ = block($2);
      }
    | LET let
      {
        $$ = $2;
      }
    | CASE expr OF branch_list ESAC
      {
        $$ = typcase($2, $4);
      }
    | NEW TYPEID
      {
        $$ = new_($2);
      }
    | ISVOID expr
      {
        $$ = isvoid($2);
      }
    | expr '+' expr
      {
        $$ = plus($1, $3);
      }
    | expr '-' expr
      {
        $$ = sub($1, $3);
      }
    | expr '*' expr
      {
        $$ = mul($1, $3);
      }
    | expr '/' expr
      {
        $$ = divide($1, $3);
      }
    | '~' expr
      {
        $$ = neg($2);
      }
    | expr '<' expr
      {
        $$ = lt($1, $3);
      }
    | expr LE expr
      {
        $$ = leq($1, $3);
      }
    | expr '=' expr
      {
        $$ = eq($1, $3);
      }
    | NOT expr
      {
        $$ = comp($2);
      }
    | '(' expr ')'
      {
        $$ = $2;
      }
    | OBJECTID
      {
        $$ = object($1);
      }
    | INT_CONST
      {
        $$ = int_const($1);
      }
    | STR_CONST
      {
        $$ = string_const($1);
      }
    | BOOL_CONST
      {
        $$ = bool_const($1);
      }
    ;

    expr_list:
      expr
      {
        $$ = single_Expressions($1);
      }
    | expr_list ',' expr
      {
        $$ = append_Expressions($1, single_Expressions($3));
      }
    ;

    expr_list_:
      expr ';'
      {
        $$ = single_Expressions($1);
      }
    | expr_list_ expr ';'
      {
        $$ = append_Expressions($1, single_Expressions($2));
      }
    | expr_list_ error ';'
      {
        $$ = $1;
      }
    ;

    let:
      OBJECTID ':' TYPEID IN expr
      {
        $$ = let($1, $3, no_expr(), $5);
      }
    | OBJECTID ':' TYPEID ',' let
      {
        $$ = let($1, $3, no_expr(), $5);
      }
    | OBJECTID ':' TYPEID ASSIGN expr IN expr
      {
        $$ = let($1, $3, $5, $7);
      }
    | OBJECTID ':' TYPEID ASSIGN expr ',' let
      {
        $$ = let($1, $3, $5, $7);
      }
    | error ',' let
      {
        $$ = $3;
      }
    ;

    branch_list:
      branch
      {
        $$ = single_Cases($1);
      }
    | branch_list branch
      {
        $$ = append_Cases($1, single_Cases($2));
      }
    ;

    branch:
      OBJECTID ':' TYPEID DARROW expr ';'
      {
        $$ = branch($1, $3, $5);
      }
    ;


    /* end of grammar */
%%
    
/* This function is called automatically when Bison detects a parse error. */
void yyerror(char *s)
{
  extern int curr_lineno;
  
  cerr << "\"" << curr_filename << "\", line " << curr_lineno << ": " \
  << s << " at or near ";
  print_cool_token(yychar);
  cerr << endl;
  omerrs++;
  
  if(omerrs>50) {fprintf(stdout, "More than 50 errors\n"); exit(1);}
}
╭─root@82017536e4ec ~/cool/assignments/PA3 
╰─# perl pa2-grading.pl 
Grading .....
make: Entering directory `/usr/class/cs143/cool/assignments/PA3'
bison -d -v -y -b cool --debug -p cool_yy cool.y
cool.y: warning: 18 shift/reduce conflicts [-Wconflicts-sr]
mv -f cool.tab.c cool-parse.cc
/bin/sh -ec 'g++ -MM -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA3 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA3 cool-parse.cc | sed '\''s/\(cool-parse\.o\)[ :]*/\1 cool-parse.d : /g'\'' > cool-parse.d'
make: Leaving directory `/usr/class/cs143/cool/assignments/PA3'
make: Entering directory `/usr/class/cs143/cool/assignments/PA3'
g++ -g -Wall -Wno-unused -Wno-deprecated -Wno-write-strings -DDEBUG -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA3 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA3 -c cool-parse.cc
g++ -g -Wall -Wno-unused -Wno-deprecated -Wno-write-strings -DDEBUG -I. -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/include/PA3 -I/usr/class/cs143/cool/src/PA3 parser-phase.o utilities.o stringtab.o dumptype.o tree.o cool-tree.o tokens-lex.o handle_flags.o cool-parse.o -lfl -o parser
make: Leaving directory `/usr/class/cs143/cool/assignments/PA3'
=====================================================================
submission: ..

=====================================================================
You got a score of 70 out of 70.

Submit code: 
70:7cbbc409ec990f19c78c75bd1e06f215

Programming Assignment 4 | 语义分析​

完成PA4需要看完Programming Assignment 3，辅助的材料依旧是Cool Reference Manual和Tour of the Cool Support Code

语义分析需要在Bison语法分析生成的AST的基础上，继续完善AST，主要任务包括

  • 查看所有的类并构建继承图
  • 检查继承图是否合法（Cool Manual 3.2）
    • 单一继承且基类必须有定义
    • 无环
  • 对每一个类进行信息收集
    • 遍历AST，收集符号中的所有的可见声明
    • 检查每个表达式的正确性
    • 用类型注释符号表

我们要在 semant.cc的ClassTable::ClassTable构造函数中编写流程，大致如下

  1. 初始化Basic Classes
  2. 初始化User Classes
  3. 检查继承是否合法
  4. 构造继承图
  5. 标记所有可以到达的节点
  6. 检查继承图中是否有环
  7. 为每个类构建Feature符号表
  8. 检查是否存在Main类以及类中是否包含main方法
  9. 检查类型是否正确

我们一共需要修改三个文件，cool-tree.handcode.h(或者直接修改cool-tree.h也行，cool-tree.h中引用了这个头文件，在这个头文件中用宏的方式添加了额外的声明和实现，比较方便)、 semant.h 、 semant.cc

最终程序 semant 由 semant-phase.cc 的 main 进入，调用 ast_root->semant() 来进行语义分析

实现细节​

我们建立了三个类Environment、InheritNode、ClassTable，三个类互相引用来实现semant

  • Environment：用来存储当前的环境，包括当前范围的 method_class 、 var_table 、class_table，以及基本的 probe 、 lookup 、enterscope 、exitscope 等方法
  • InheritNode：类继承图的基本节点，每一个 InheritNode 都与 .cl 文件中的类绑定，同时每个 InheritNode 都存在一个指向 Environment 的指针成员
  • ClassTable：继承 InheritNode 的映射表，用于实现继承图

步骤3需要检查继承是否合法，根据Cool语言中的定义

引用

There is a distinguished class Object. If a class definition does not specify a parent class, then the class inherits from Object by default. A class may inherit only from a single class; this is aptly called “single inheritance.”2 The parent-child relation on classes defines a graph. This graph may not contain cycles. For example, if C inherits from P, then P must not inherit from C. Furthermore, if C inherits from P, then P must have a class definition somewhere in the program. Because Cool has single inheritance, it follows that if both of these restrictions are satisfied, then the inheritance graph forms a tree with Object as the root.

Cool语言不允许继承未定义的类，且类必须能够继承（Cool语言中基本类只有 Object、IO 可以被继承，Bool 和 Str 是不能被继承的）

步骤6检查继承图是否包含环的时候，我们只需要检查是否有 InheritNode 是否不可到达即可。因为Cool语言是单一继承的，假如我们现在有如下的继承图

在遍历过程中，我们是从 Object 开始自顶向下遍历子节点，如果出现不可到达的情况，说明有节点并没有继承 Object，单一形成了新的AST，即存在环

步骤7中我们建立了每个类的Feature表，我们从 Object 开始，将 InheritNode 中类内的所有 attribute 和 method 都添加进表中，当父节点的Feature表建立完之后，子节点复制父节点的 Environment ，这样子节点就包含了父节点Feature表中所有的内容，然后再将子节点的 attribute 和 method 添加进表中，递归地完成了Feature表的建立

代码
  • cool-tree.handcode.h
